memoryArtifacts.headline: Lord Richard Bigge Biography
memoryArtifacts.description:
RICHARD BIGGE (1424-1474)
AGNES HENLEY (1426-1480)

RICHARD BIGGE was born 1 April 1424, Benenden, Kent, England, to Roger Bigge (1395-1472) and Joan Kent (1400-1472.) He married Joan Henley about 144 …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Bigge於1424年4月1日出生時，他的父親Lord Roger Bigge29歲，母親Joan Plantagenent24歲。他於大約1448年與Agnes Henley在英國肯特貝嫩登結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1474年6月14日享年50歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特貝嫩登聖 格奧智也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Bigge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Bigg
meanings: English: from Middle English bigge ‘large, strong, stout’, presumably a descriptive nickname for a large, strong person.
English: topographic name from Middle English big ‘building’, a derivative of Middle English biggen (Old Norse byggja) ‘to build’.
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form of German   Bick   or of some other similar (like-sounding) name from continental Europe (compare   Big   2).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Bigge was born on 1 April 1424, his father, Lord Roger Bigge, was 29 and his mother, Joan Plantagenent, was 24. He married Agnes Henley about 1448, in Benenden, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 14 June 1474, at the age of 50, and was buried in St George's Church, Benenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Agnes Henley environ 1448, à Benenden, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 14 juin 1474, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à St George's Church, Benenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Biggeが1424年4月1日に生まれた時，父Lord Roger Biggeは29歳，母Joan Plantagenentは24歳であった。約1448年にイギリスケントベンエンデンでAgnes Henleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1474年6月14日に50歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントベンエンデンスト ジョージェ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Bigge(이)가 1424년 4월 1일에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Roger Bigge(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Plantagenent(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1448년 영국 켄트 베니든에서 Agnes Henley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1474년 6월 14일 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 베니든 스트 조그'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Bigge nasceu em 1 de abril de 1424, seu pai, Lord Roger Bigge, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Joan Plantagenent, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Henley aproximadamente 1448, em Benenden, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 14 de junho de 1474, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em St George's Church, Benenden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Saint George's Church, Benenden, Tunbrid: ст джеорге'с чурч, биненден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Bigge родился 1 апреля 1424 г., его отцу по имени Lord Roger Bigge было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Plantagenent, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Henley около 1448 г. в местности биненден, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 14 июня 1474 г. в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности ст джеорге'с чурч, биненден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
